items.nearby_content:
Abstract
This  paper  presents  analysis  of  two-stage  grid-tied  Photovoltaic  (PV)  microinverter, confirming its designing, modelling and performance improvements  reducing Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) by IEEE power quality standards. The  study evaluates three various filter configurations integrated into microinverter,  each  designed  to  mitigate  THD  in  the  grid  current,  the  first  configuration  implement  LCL  filter,  obtaining  THD  of  approximately  11%,  the  second  configuration introduces hybrid filtering approach that combines the LCL filter with  hybrid Half Bridge (H-B) filter, demonstrating improvements and yielding THD  values between 6-7%, this combination efficient addresses high frequency noise,  enhancing output quality, the third configuration utilizes hybrid LCL and Passive  Damping (PD) filter, this developed arrangement results in the lowest THD levels,  reaching as low as 3-4%, the passive damping component stabilizes the system  by  mitigating  resonances  and  improving  performance  at  varying  operation  conditions, Experimental results reflect steady-state operation verification through  Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) testing using (PLE
